metrics.narrative: SGD's pain is well documented operationally but never converted into a buyer-owned dollar figure. On the 20 Jun 2025 discovery call Bihag Shah described manual order-acknowledgment follow-ups caused by thin European plant staffing, and on 9 Sep 2025 SGD's IT lead flagged frustration with duplicate SAP/legal-tool data entry from the lack of a centralized contract repository. The only quantified savings figures on record (~40% time savings, ~35% cost reduction) were presented by Zycus during the 28 Oct 2025 demo as our own value proposition, not a customer-validated business case.

champion.narrative: Bihag Shah, Group Purchasing Director, has championed Zycus since the June 2025 discovery call and actively fought internally for a full-suite award even after Legal chose a rival for CLM, telling Pierre Meraud on 29 Jan 2026 that losing CLM 'doesn't discard you from the remaining purchasing modules' and that he personally pushed leadership to keep Zycus in play. He has proactively kept Zycus updated by text/call through the freeze (Next_Step, 9 and 14 Apr 2026) rather than going silent. What is missing: his advocacy has not yet secured any new budget date, and his own influence is constrained pending September 2026's FY27 re-ask.

competition.narrative: The CLM module was formally awarded to an unnamed niche CLM vendor by 6 Feb 2026, on a roughly $160k lower three-year TCO and a marginally stronger functional/AI fit (98% vs Zycus's ~90%), per Bihag Shah on the 29 Jan 2026 negotiation call. Salesforce's Competitors__c field additionally lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua and JAGGAER as tracked alternatives, though none surfaced by name in any call read this cycle. Zycus remains the lead option for the non-CLM S2P scope (sourcing, eProcurement, supplier onboarding) if and when the project resumes.

identify_pain.narrative: Buyer-articulated pain is specific and consistent: contracts tracked in Word/Excel templates with no centralized repository or renewal alerts (Avoma, 9 Sep 2025, buyer IT), manual RFQ/PO handling via email and Excel across thinly staffed European plants (Avoma, 20 Jun 2025, Bihag Shah), and supplier onboarding cited as taking up to two months manually (Avoma, 29 Oct 2025). The pain owner is clearly Bihag Shah's purchasing organization, though it has not yet been urgent enough to protect FY26 budget against the Contract Management delay.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, or SOW has been executed. Zycus discussed and began preparing OF1/OF2 drafts and an MSA in late Jan 2026 ('Need to send MSA and OF1/2 first week of feb' - Next_Step, 27 Jan 2026), but this stalled once Legal awarded CLM elsewhere and the FY26 S2P budget was withdrawn. Paper process would need to restart from scratch if and when FY27 budget clears.

economic_buyer.narrative: Philippe Pourquèry, Group CFO, is the confirmed economic buyer per Salesforce MEDDPICC and corroborated on calls: Bihag Shah told Zycus on 29 Jan 2026 he would 'be meeting Philip personally tomorrow again, our group CFO, who also heads IT' to seek full-suite approval, and CFO sign-off was reportedly obtained in principle around that time. However, the FY26 budget he had provisionally cleared was later withdrawn (Next_Step, 27 Feb and 14 Apr 2026) due to a delayed Contract Management rollout and the private-equity owner's sale plans. There is no record of direct Zycus contact with Philippe since Jan 2026; he is currently disengaged pending the FY27 re-ask.

decision_process.narrative: The process split into two tracks: a CLM-only track owned by Legal (Valérie Breon-Normand) that concluded with the niche competitor's selection around 6 Feb 2026, and a broader S2P track requiring CFO and, ultimately, PE-owner PAI Partners' capex approval. Both tracks stalled at the 27 Feb 2026 'Validation of your S2P project' call, where Bihag disclosed the project is postponed indefinitely. The next process gate is Bihag's planned September 2026 attempt to re-secure FY27 budget (Next_Step, 15 Jun 2026), with no confirmed date.

decision_criteria.narrative: For CLM, Bihag Shah stated on 29 Jan 2026 that the niche competitor covered roughly 98% of requirements versus Zycus's ~90%, and that the price gap (roughly $160k over three years) made Zycus unjustifiable for that module alone. For the broader S2P scope, criteria centered on SAP on-prem integration architecture, Salesforce native connectivity, security/DR posture, and license tiering, all deeply probed in the 19 and 28 Jan 2026 technical workshops. These criteria have not been re-tested since the project froze in Feb 2026.

items.summary: Pierre wished Bihag a good summer break and suggested restarting discussions in September or October, flagging his own out-of-office from mid-August to September 7th; no immediate action requested, so the deal effectively goes dormant until reconnection.

day_summary.overall: Pierre proactively reached out to pause the conversation for summer, proposing to pick discussions back up in September or October rather than letting it go cold silently. It's a courteous check-in with no active ask, but it effectively pushes any real progress out by two to three months and puts the onus on Bihag to re-engage first when back from break.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus lost the CLM module to an unnamed niche CLM vendor by 6 Feb 2026 on price (roughly $160k lower 3-year TCO) and a marginally better functional/AI fit (98% vs ~90%), per Bihag Shah's own account on the 29 Jan 2026 negotiation call.

bullets.full: Zycus's 3-year CLM TCO (€360k for 200 power users) is €160k higher than the niche competitor (€200k), a gap Bihag stated on 29 Jan 2026 he could not justify to management ('with the gap of price today, it's not possible for me to push that'). Zycus delivered a revised dual offer (CLM-only vs full S2P) on 30 Jan 2026 targeting ~€250k 3-year S2P TCO, but legal had already selected the niche CLM player by 6 Feb 2026. If the deal resumes and SGD re-opens S2P, pricing for the broader suite (sourcing + eProcurement + supplier onboarding + CLM or excluding CLM) must land below €300k 3-year to be competitive per Bihag's 29 Jan guidance.
bullets.text: Zycus's 3-year CLM TCO (€360k for 200 power users) is €160k higher than the niche competitor (€200k), a gap Bihag stated on 29 Jan 2026 he could not justify to…

bullets.full: Legal (Valérie, Group General Counsel) is the decision-maker for CLM and selected the niche competitor in early Feb 2026 over Zycus (€100k cheaper, better AI per Bihag). Purchasing (Bihag, Fiona) advocated for Zycus on the full S2P platform integration argument, but legal's preference prevailed for the CLM module. If SGD unbundles the S2P award (CLM to niche player, sourcing/eProcurement/supplier onboarding to Zycus), legal's lack of sponsorship for Zycus creates political friction and may slow contract negotiations or IT alignment. Mitigation: if deal resumes, position the S2P modules (excluding CLM) as purchasing-led with IT validation (already done 28 Jan 2026), minimizing legal's veto power.

bullets.full: Close date is 17 Mar 2027 (253 days out), but the deal has been frozen since 27 Feb 2026 with no new project timeline from the buyer. Bihag stated on 15 Jun 2026 he will ask for S2P re-budget approval in Sep 2026 at earliest (Next_Step 15 Jun 2026: 'Ask Bihag if he will ask for approval of this project again in September'). If the private-equity owner's sale of SGD completes in 2026 or early 2027, the new owner may greenlight S2P within first 90 days post-close OR kill the project entirely; Zycus has no visibility into the M&A timeline or the new owner's capex priorities. The current close date is a placeholder; realistic restart is Q4 2026 at earliest if budget approved, pushing go-live to mid-2027 or later.

Ownership freeze, not execution gap — The deal is not dark due to lack of champion, EB access, or competitive positioning; it is frozen by the private-equity owner's pending sale of SGD Pharma and 2026 capex freeze (Bihag, 14 Apr 2026). Standard deal-recovery plays (re-engage champion, map new power, run executive connect) will not unblock this; the move is to stay warm with Bihag and request notification when the ownership decision resolves and S2P re-budgeting discussion begins (likely Sep 2026 at earliest per Next_Step 15 Jun 2026 note). Monitor for M&A close announcement; if SGD is sold, the new owner may greenlight or kill S2P within first 90 days.

Maintain warm quarterly touch with Bihag through 2026 — Bihag (CPO, champion) remains the internal advocate and will drive re-budgeting when the ownership freeze lifts. The risk is Zycus goes silent for 6+ months and Bihag loses confidence or re-opens vendor selection. Recommend quarterly low-touch value-add: (1) Q3 2026 (Sep): check in on ownership decision timeline and S2P re-budget discussion; share a Merlin AI use case or procurement-transformation benchmark relevant to pharma/manufacturing. (2) Q4 2026 (Dec): if no movement, invite Bihag to a virtual Horizon session or customer round-table (peer pharma CPO discussing S2P ROI). (3) Q1 2027 (Mar, near the current close date): request a re-planning call if budget is approved; if not, propose deferring close date to Q3 2027 and keeping the opportunity warm rather than marking lost. Goal: stay top-of-mind without being pushy; let Bihag know Zycus is ready to move fast when SGD is.
